musl_c23_compat.c(3) Library Functions Manual musl_c23_compat.c(3)

NAME

musl_c23_compat.c - 🔄 musl C23 compatibility wrappers for __isoc23_* symbol aliases (glibc 2.38+ compatibility)

SYNOPSIS

Functions


long musl_strtol (const char *, extern long long musl_strtoll(const char char **, int)
long long __isoc23_strtoll (const char *str, char **endptr, int base)
Convert string to long long (C23 compatible). unsigned long __isoc23_strtoul (const char *str, char **endptr, int base)
Convert string to unsigned long (C23 compatible). unsigned long long __isoc23_strtoull (const char *str, char **endptr, int base)
Convert string to unsigned long long (C23 compatible). long __isoc23_wcstol (const wchar_t *str, wchar_t **endptr, int base)
Wide string to integer conversion. long long __isoc23_wcstoll (const wchar_t *str, wchar_t **endptr, int base)
Convert wide string to long long (C23 compatible). unsigned long __isoc23_wcstoul (const wchar_t *str, wchar_t **endptr, int base)
Convert wide string to unsigned long (C23 compatible). unsigned long long __isoc23_wcstoull (const wchar_t *str, wchar_t **endptr, int base)
Convert wide string to unsigned long long (C23 compatible). int __isoc23_scanf (const char *format,...)
Formatted input functions. int __isoc23_fscanf (FILE *stream, const char *format,...)
Read formatted input from file stream (C23 compatible). int __isoc23_sscanf (const char *str, const char *format,...)
Read formatted input from string (C23 compatible). int __isoc23_vscanf (const char *format, va_list args)
Read formatted input from stdin (va_list variant, C23 compatible). int __isoc23_vfscanf (FILE *stream, const char *format, va_list args)
Read formatted input from file stream (va_list variant, C23 compatible). int __isoc23_vsscanf (const char *str, const char *format, va_list args)
Read formatted input from string (va_list variant, C23 compatible). int __isoc23_wscanf (const wchar_t *format,...)
Wide character formatted input functions. int __isoc23_fwscanf (FILE *stream, const wchar_t *format,...)
Read formatted wide input from file stream (C23 compatible). int __isoc23_swscanf (const wchar_t *str, const wchar_t *format,...)
Read formatted wide input from string (C23 compatible). int __isoc23_vwscanf (const wchar_t *format, va_list args)
Read formatted wide input from stdin (va_list variant, C23 compatible). int __isoc23_vfwscanf (FILE *stream, const wchar_t *format, va_list args)
Read formatted wide input from file stream (va_list variant, C23 compatible). int __isoc23_vswscanf (const wchar_t *str, const wchar_t *format, va_list args)
Read formatted wide input from string (va_list variant, C23 compatible). void arc4random_buf (void *buf, size_t nbytes)
glibc-specific random functions for expat and fontconfig compatibility int initstate_r (unsigned int seed, char *statebuf, size_t statelen, struct random_data *buf)
Initialize reentrant random state (glibc compatibility stub for musl). int random_r (struct random_data *restrict buf, int32_t *restrict result)
Generate cryptographically secure random number (glibc compatibility stub for musl).

Detailed Description

🔄 musl C23 compatibility wrappers for __isoc23_* symbol aliases (glibc 2.38+ compatibility)

Note: These aliases provide the base functionality but don't implement the full C23 binary literal support (0b/0B prefix) until musl adds native support.

References:

  • https://reviews.llvm.org/D158943
  • https://groups.google.com/g/osv-dev/c/zDx0qThbtEE

Definition in file musl_c23_compat.c.

Function Documentation

int __isoc23_fscanf (FILE * stream, const char * format, ...)

Read formatted input from file stream (C23 compatible).

Parameters

stream File stream
format Format string
... Variable arguments

Returns

Number of successfully read items

Definition at line 129 of file musl_c23_compat.c.

129                                                            {
130   va_list args;
131   va_start(args, format);
132   int result = vfscanf(stream, format, args);
133   va_end(args);
134   return result;
135 }

base Numeric base (0-36)

int
  initstate_r (unsigned int seed, char * statebuf, size_t statelen, struct
  random_data * buf)


Initialize reentrant random state (glibc compatibility stub for
    musl). musl doesn't provide this glibc-specific reentrant random function.
    Used by fontconfig for thread-local hash seeding during initialization.








Design
  Notes


This is a stub implementation (doesn't maintain stateful PRNG
    state) for these reasons:


    
  
  • fontconfig only calls this once per thread during startup (not in hot
      loops)
    • 
  
  • The performance difference between a syscall and PRNG state lookup is
      negligible at initialization time
    • 
  
  • Implementing a correct PRNG is complex; using kernel randomness is simpler
      and more reliable than maintaining application-level state
    • 







Why
  the state buffer is not used


    
  
  • glibc's stateful design (storing PRNG state in statebuf) is an
      optimization for applications that call random_r() thousands of
      times
    • 
  
  • fontconfig doesn't fit that pattern; it just needs a few random values at
      init
    • 
  
  • Maintaining thread-local state would require either TLS allocation or
      synchronization
    • 
  
  • Not worth the complexity for initialization-time randomness
    • 







Thread
  Safety


Thread-safe by design:


    
  
  • Each thread can have its own buf/statebuf
    • 
  
  • Each thread's random_r() calls arc4random_buf(), which is
      atomic at kernel level
    • 
  
  • No shared mutable state means no synchronization needed
    • 



Parameters


seed Seed value (ignored; random_r() uses
  arc4random_buf())


statebuf State buffer (validated but not used)


statelen Length of state buffer (must be >= 32)


buf Random data structure (validated but not used)


Returns


0 on success, -1 if parameters invalid


Definition at line 346 of file
  musl_c23_compat.c.


346                                                                                              {
347   (void)seed;
348   // Validate parameters match glibc interface
349   if (!statebuf || !buf || statelen < 32) {
350     return -1;
351   }
352 
353   // Initialize buffer (though random_r() doesn't actually use it)
354   memset(statebuf, 0, statelen);
355 
356   return 0;
357 }










long
  musl_strtol (const char * , extern long long musl_strtoll(const char char **,
  int ) [extern]


Definition at line 23 of file musl_c23_compat.c.


31                                                                {
32   return musl_strtol(str, endptr, base);
33 }










int
  random_r (struct random_data *restrict buf, int32_t *restrict result)


Generate cryptographically secure random number (glibc
    compatibility stub for musl). musl doesn't provide this glibc-specific
    reentrant random function. Used by fontconfig to generate thread-safe random
    numbers without shared state.








Design


    
  
  • Ignores the state buffer (buf) entirely
    • 
  
  • Each call invokes arc4random_buf() to get fresh random bytes from
      kernel
    • 
  
  • Thread-safe because arc4random_buf() uses atomic syscalls/device
      reads
    • 
  
  • No per-thread PRNG state to maintain or synchronize
    • 







Why
  stateless design


    
  
  • fontconfig's usage pattern doesn't warrant state management (see
      initstate_r())
    • 
  
  • Stateless design trades a syscall per call for simplicity and
    correctness
    • 
  
  • Kernel randomness is reliable; application-level PRNG state would need
      testing
    • 
  
  • See initstate_r() for full rationale on not maintaining state
    • 



Parameters


buf Random data structure (ignored; present for
  glibc API compatibility)


result Pointer to store generated random value in range [0, 2^31)


Returns


0 on success, -1 if buf or result is NULL


Definition at line 383 of file
  musl_c23_compat.c.


383                                                                          {
384   if (buf == NULL || result == NULL) {
385     return -1;
386   }
387 
388   /* Obtain cryptographically secure random bytes from kernel */
389   uint32_t random_bytes;
390   arc4random_buf(&random_bytes, sizeof(random_bytes));
391 
392   /* Convert to 31-bit positive integer matching glibc behavior
393      glibc's random_r returns values in range [0, 2^31) */
394   *result = (int32_t)((random_bytes >> 1) & 0x7FFFFFFF);
395 
396   return 0;
397 }






References arc4random_buf().








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>
